functions.name: CNContactStore.RequestAccesss
functions.description: Request access to the user's contacts. Users are able to grant or deny access to contact data on a per-application basis. To request access to contact data, call CNContactStore.RequestAccesss function. This will not block the application while the user is being asked to grant or deny access. The user will only be prompted the first time access is requested; any subsequent ContactStore calls will use the existing permissions. Later you can use CNContactStore.AuthorizationStatus to check status or CNContactStore.AuthorizationError to learn why you didn't get permissions. The application needs to be code signed with entitlements with com.apple.security.personal-information.addressbook identifier. See also my idea here: https://community.filemaker.com/ideas/3240

parameters.name: Wait
parameters.description: Pass 1 to wait for user to decide or 0 to return immediately. Default is 0.

examples.label: Request access:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "CNContactStore.RequestAccesss" ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CNContactStore.RequestAccesss" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Request authorization with some error checking:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "CNContactStore.RequestAccesss" ) ]
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): 1 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
CNContactStore.AuthorizationError
" ) ]
If [ Length(
$r
) > 0 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Error" ;
$r
]
End If
Set Variable [
$groups
; Value:
MBS
( "
CNContactStore.Groups
" ) ]
If [ Length (
$groups
) = 0 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Possible Problem" ; "Either you have no groups or you did not code sign the app?" ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CNContactStore.RequestAccesss" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CNContactStore.AuthorizationError" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> Length($r) > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Error" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CNContactStore.Groups" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$groups</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> Length ( $groups ) = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Possible Problem" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"Either you have no groups or you did not code sign the app?" </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CNContactStore.UpdateContact
functions.description: Updates a contact. After you set values with CNContact.SetValue , you can make changes permanent here. You may get Cocoa error 134092 if the contact has a note field entry since FileMaker is not allowed to read/write the note field.

parameters.name: ContactIdentifier
parameters.description: The contact identifier.

examples.label: Update a contact by adding an URL:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$ContactID
; Value: "BD2307E5-92F6-4A8F-946D-C4804615C18B:ABPerson" ]
# add the URL:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
CNContact.AddURLAddress
";
$contactID
; "http://www.monkeybreadsoftware.com/"; "Homepage" ) ]
# and save
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "CNContactStore.UpdateContact";
$ContactID
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"BD2307E5-92F6-4A8F-946D-C4804615C18B:ABPerson" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ContactID</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and save</Text>
  </Step>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CNContactStore.UpdateContact"; $ContactID ) </Calculation>

    </Repetition>

functions.name: Debugger.CurrentLineNumber
functions.description: Queries current line number. This is the line number showing the hash mark and may not be the current line. If FileMaker does not update the debugger window while running several steps, the line returned is usually the last one it stopped. Result is -1 if not known. First row is number 1.

functions.name: Debugger.GetCallStack
functions.description: Queries current call stack from debugger panel. Usually first entry is current script followed by callers. Useful for automatic debugging with hotkey functions. This function checks for a license. Created 14th August 2017 , last changed 11st December 2023 Debugger.CurrentLineNumber - Debugger.IsDataViewerVisible 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: DocumentPicker.Export
functions.description: Shows dialog to export files. The user selects an external destination. The document picker copies the document, leaving the original unchanged. On success, we store the list of file paths and trigger script. Script is trigger with parameter "Pick" or "Cancel". The file paths refer to new copies of the exported documents at the selected destination. Warning: Function returns immediately and dialog shows after function returned asynchronously.

parameters.name: Paths
parameters.description: Native file path list for the documents to pass.

examples.label: Export a temp file:
examples.text_code:
# build a native path:
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
MBS
( "
Folders.UserTemporary
" ); "test.txt") ]
# write a text file
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Text.WriteTextFile
"; "Hello World";
$path
; "UTF-8") ]
# and show export dialog:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "DocumentPicker.Export";
$path
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; MBS( "Folders.UserTemporary" ); "test.txt") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> write a text file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.WriteTextFile"; "Hello World"; $path; "UTF-8") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and show export dialog:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DocumentPicker.Export"; $path ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

functions.name: DocumentPicker.File
functions.description: Queries native file path for a file. Pass index from 0 to DocumentPicker.FileCount -1. Example path you may see for import: "/Containers/Data/Application/2E993ED8-F5AA-4A18-877C-F0CB72485337/tmp/de.monkeybreadsoftware.test-Inbox/IMG_0005.JPG" And for open without inbox:

functions.name: DocumentPicker.Files
functions.description: Queries list of native file paths.

examples.label: Sample trigger script:
examples.text_code:
Set Field [
PlaceHolder::Paths
;
MBS
( "DocumentPicker.Files" ) ]
Set Variable [
$path
; Value: GetValue(
PlaceHolder::Paths
; 1) ]
If [ Length (
$path
) > 0 ]
Set Field [
PlaceHolder::Container
;
MBS
( "
Container.ReadFile
";
$path
; "auto") ]
Else
Set Field [
PlaceHolder::Container
; "" ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "DocumentPicker.Files" ) </Calculation>
    <Field table="PlaceHolder" id="99999" name="Paths "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetValue(PlaceHolder::Paths; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> Length ( $path ) > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "Container.ReadFile"; $path; "auto") </Calculation>
    <Field table="PlaceHolder" id="99999" name="Container "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>"" </Calculation>
    <Field table="PlaceHolder" id="99999" name="Container "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DocumentPicker.Import
functions.description: Shows dialog to import files. The user selects an external document. The document picker copies the document, leaving the original unchanged. On success, we store the list of file paths and trigger script. Script is trigger with parameter "Pick" or "Cancel". The path list refers to a copy of the selected documents. These documents are temporary files. They remain available only until your application terminates. To keep a permanent copy, move these files to a permanent location inside your sandbox. Warning: Function returns immediately and dialog shows after function returned asynchronously.

parameters.name: AllowedUTIs
parameters.description: The list of allowed universal types.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various flags. Value 1: Whether to allow multiple files to be selected.  (iOS 11 or newer, otherwise ignored) Value 2: Whether to show file extensions (iOS 13 or newer, otherwise ignored) You can combine both flags and pass 1+2.

parameters.name: DirectoryPath
parameters.description: The path to the directory to use as base. New in iOS 13.

examples.label: Import file with DocumentPicker:
examples.text_code:

Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
DocumentPicker.SetTrigger
"; Get(FileName); "Trigger" ) ]
# allowed list of file types:
Set Variable [
$Types
; Value: "public.jpeg¶public.png" ]
# show import dialog:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "DocumentPicker.Import";
$Types
; 0 ) ]

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> allowed list of file types:</Text>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>"public.jpeg¶public.png" </Calculation>

    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Types</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> show import dialog:</Text>

functions.name: DocumentPicker.Move

functions.name: DocumentPicker.Open

On success, we store the list of file paths and trigger script. Script is trigger with parameter "Pick" or "Cancel". The paths list refers to the selected documents. Warning: Function returns immediately and dialog shows after function returned asynchronously. For FileMaker database files, please use com.filemaker.doc.fmp12 as UTI or simply public.data.

Set Field [

;

DocumentPicker.Files

; Value: GetValue(

If [ Length (

MBS

$path

  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>"" </Calculation>
    <Field table="PlaceHolder" id="99999" name="Container "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Overlay.AddWebViewer
functions.description: Adds a web viewer to an overlay. The web pages loaded can define a transparent background to make the whole page transparent. Then you can have things on top, that can have round borders or other transparency effects. e.g. the body may be set to style="background:rgba(255, 255, 255, 0); margin: 0; height: 100%; overflow: hidden;" and a div in front of it could have round rectangles and a red background with style="border-radius: 15px; background:rgba(255, 100, 100, 1); height:100px; padding:5px;". If the loaded website has no background, mouse clicks may fall through. This is similar to WebView.CreateWithControl and creates the same web viewer on the overlay. You can fmp: URLs and use FileMaker.PerformScript just like FileMaker's web viewer. For FileMaker.PerformScriptWithOption the option can be 1 for halt, 2 for exit, 3 for resume, 4 for pause. 0 is pause instead of continue and 6 is resume instead of Suspend and Resume since the plugin can't do these modes. e.g. trigger a script: FileMaker.PerformScript( "Script name", "Optional Parameter"); You can use FileMaker.Evaluate to directly evaluate something from JavaScript. e.g. query a get function like this: let filename = await FileMaker.Evaluate('Get(FileName)');

parameters.name: OverlayRef
parameters.description: The overlay window reference number.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Pass 1 to allow triggering scripts from JavaScript. Pass 2 to allow FileMaker.Evaluate in JavaScript. Pass 3 to allow both. Default is 0.

functions.result: Returns webviewer reference number or error.

examples.label: Creates an overlay with a web viewer and load an URL into it:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$overlay
; Value:
MBS
("
Overlay.Create
") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Overlay.SetFrame
";
$overlay
; 100; 100; 300; 120) ]
Set Variable [
$$web
; Value:
MBS
("Overlay.AddWebViewer";
$overlay
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
WebView.LoadURL
";
$$web
; "https://monkeybreadsoftware.com/filemaker/examples/transparent.html") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Overlay.SetVisible
";
$overlay
; 1) ]
Set Variable [
$$overlay
; Value:
$overlay
]

    </Value>

    </Repetition>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

      <Calculation>$overlay </Calculation>
    </Value>

    </Repetition>

functions.name: Overlay.SetMouseClickScript
functions.description: Sets the script to run on a mouse click. Triggered if the user clicks, but does not drag the window. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: OverlayRef
parameters.description: The overlay window reference number.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: Value

functions.name: Overlay.SetTag
functions.description: Sets tag for window. This allows you to associate any value with the overlay window, e.g. a record ID.

parameters.name: OverlayRef
parameters.description: The overlay window reference number.

parameters.name: Tag
parameters.description: The new tag value. Empty text if not provided.

functions.name: Overlay.SetTitle
functions.description: Sets the window title. The title is not shown, but may show up when windows get listed somewhere.

parameters.name: OverlayRef
parameters.description: The overlay window reference number.

functions.name: Overlay.SetTopMost
functions.description: Sets window to be topmost. If window is topmost, it floats over all other windows.

parameters.name: OverlayRef
parameters.description: The overlay window reference number.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new topmost setting. Can be 0 or 1.

functions.name: Overlay.SetVisible
functions.description: Sets whether the window is visible.

parameters.name: OverlayRef
parameters.description: The overlay window reference number.

parameters.name: Visible
parameters.description: Pass 1 to make window visible or 0 to hide it.

functions.name: Socket.AcceptNewConnection
functions.description: Accepts a new connection and returns a new socket.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

functions.result: Returns new Socket ID or error message.

examples.label: In the script triggered for a new connection, connect a new socket:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$sock
; Value:Get(ScriptParameter)]
Set Variable [
$newSock
; Value:
MBS
("Socket.AcceptNewConnection";
$sock
)]
If [GetAsNumber(
$newSock
)>0]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Socket.SetErrorHandler
";
$newsock
; Get(FileName); "SocketError")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Socket.SetDataAvailableHandler
";
$newsock
; Get(FileName); "ReadSomething")]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Get(ScriptParameter)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sock</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Socket.AcceptNewConnection"; $sock)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$newSock</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>GetAsNumber($newSock)>0</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Socket.SetErrorHandler"; $newsock; Get(FileName); "SocketError")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Socket.SetDataAvailableHandler"; $newsock; Get(FileName); "ReadSomething")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Socket.AddMembership
functions.description: Adds this socket to a multicast group. Internally this uses IPPROTO_IP/IP_ADD_MEMBERSHIP option. Lasterror is set.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

parameters.name: MultiAddress
parameters.description: The IPv4 address of the gorup to join.

parameters.name: InterfaceAddress
parameters.description: The IPv4 address of the network interface to use. Can be empty for any interface.

functions.name: Socket.AvailableBytes
functions.description: Queries number of bytes in read buffer. May not work for SSL sockets. The buffer size of the socket is managed by the OS and can be several megabytes big if needed. Changed in v10.4 to return error if socket is closed or disconnected instead of zero.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

examples.label: Query available byte count:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Socket.AvailableBytes"; $sock )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Socket.Check
functions.description: Performs the socket event check. The plugin checks regularly if there are new events for the socket and calls the right scripts. In a tight loop you can call this method to check sockets and have events dispatched. Normally you have no need to call this function.

functions.name: Socket.CheckAll
functions.description: Checks status of all sockets. Returns a list of sockets and what to do. The list is always build using a reference number of a socket followed by an entry with the status. Status can be error, read, write, or accept. So when you get a socket with read status, you can read data from it. With write status you can write some data to it. With accept, you can start a new connection with the incoming request. In case of error status, the plugin closes the socket automatically. Returns empty text if there is nothing to do.

functions.name: Socket.ClearDataAvailableHandler
functions.description: Clears the data available handler.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

functions.name: Socket.ClearErrorHandler
functions.description: Clears the error handler.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

functions.name: Socket.ClearNewConnectionHandler
functions.description: Clears the new connection handler.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

functions.name: Socket.Close
functions.description: Closes a socket.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

examples.label: Disconnect socket:
examples.text_code:
If [IsEmpty(
$$sock
)]
Else
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Socket.Close";
$$Sock
)]
Set Variable [
$$Sock
; Value:""]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [IsEmpty($$sock)]
Else
	Set Variable [$r; Value:MBS("Socket.Close"; $$Sock)]
	Set Variable [$$Sock; Value:""]
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Socket.CloseAll
functions.description: Closes all open sockets.

functions.name: Socket.Connect
functions.description: Connects a TCP/IP socket to the given domain/IP on the given port. You need to call Socket.Close later to release memory used for this socket. Version 6.3 now supports passing IPv4, iPv6, or a host name. For macOS the application may need to have permissions for local network access. You may get an error message like "Socket connect failed with error 65: No route to host". Check system preferences application and allow local network for FileMaker Pro in the privacy section.

parameters.name: IPorDomain
parameters.description: The IP or domain. For a dommain name, the DNS server is queried to find an IP address.

parameters.name: Port
parameters.description: The desired network port to connect to.

parameters.name: TimeOut
parameters.description: The connection timeout in seconds. Default depends on operation system and can be e.g. 90 seconds.

parameters.name: ProtocolPreference
parameters.description: Pass 4 to connect via IPv4. Pass 6 to connect via IPv6. Default is to try whatever the DNS suggests first.

functions.result: Returns socket ID for other functions or error message.

examples.label: Connect if needed and send something:
examples.text_code:
# connect if needed
If [IsEmpty(
$$sock
)]
Set Variable [
$$sock
; Value:
MBS
("Socket.Connect";
TCP Sender::TargetIP
;
TCP Sender::Port
)]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Set Field [
TCP Sender::Result
; "Failed to create socket: " &
$$sock
]
Exit Script []
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Socket.SetDataAvailableHandler
";
$$sock
; Get(FileName); "DataAvailable")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Socket.SetErrorHandler
";
$$sock
; Get(FileName); "SocketError")]
End If
# send some data
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Socket.Write
";
$$sock
;
TCP Sender::Message
; "UTF-8")]
If [GetAsNumber(
$r
) = 0]
Set Field [
TCP Sender::Result
; "Failed to Send: "&
$r
]
Else
Set Field [
TCP Sender::Result
; "Sent "&
$r
&" Bytes."]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> connect if needed</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>IsEmpty($$sock)</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Socket.Connect"; TCP Sender::TargetIP; TCP Sender::Port)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$$sock</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("IsError")</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>"Failed to create socket: " & $$sock</Calculation>
    <Field table="TCP Sender" id="99999" name="Result"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Socket.SetDataAvailableHandler"; $$sock; Get(FileName); "DataAvailable")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Socket.SetErrorHandler"; $$sock; Get(FileName); "SocketError")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> send some data</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Socket.Write"; $$sock; TCP Sender::Message; "UTF-8")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>GetAsNumber($r) = 0</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>"Failed to Send: "&$r</Calculation>
    <Field table="TCP Sender" id="99999" name="Result"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>"Sent "&$r&" Bytes."</Calculation>
    <Field table="TCP Sender" id="99999" name="Result"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Connect to IP and send command and wait for answer:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$sock
; Value:
MBS
("Socket.Connect";
test::IP
; 16128)]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Failed to connect"]
Else
Set Variable [
$write
; Value:
MBS
("
Socket.WriteHex
";
$sock
;
test::query
)]
Pause/Resume Script [Duration (seconds): 1]
Set Field [
test::response
;
MBS
("
Socket.ReadHex
";
$sock
; 20)]
Show Custom Dialog ["Hex: " &
test::response
& ¶ &  "Text: " &
MBS
( "
Text.DecodeFromHex
";
test::response
)]
Set Variable [
$sock
; Value:
MBS
("
Socket.Close
";
$sock
)]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Socket.Connect";test::IP; 16128)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sock</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("IsError")</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Failed to connect"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Socket.WriteHex"; $sock; test::query)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$write</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>1</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("Socket.ReadHex"; $sock; 20)</Calculation>
    <Field table="test" id="99999" name="response"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Hex: " & test::response & ¶ &  "Text: " & MBS( "Text.DecodeFromHex"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>test::response)</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Socket.Close"; $sock)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sock</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

]

; 10) ]

Loop
#  Wait for Answer
Set Variable [
$data
; Value: "" ]
Loop
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,01 ]
Set Variable [
$newdata
; Value:
MBS
("
Socket.Read
";
$sock
; 1000) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Set Variable [
$data
; Value:
$data
&
$newData
]

Exit Loop If [ Position(
$Data
; "¶"; 1; 1)>0 ]

Set Variable [
$data
; Value:
MBS
("
Text.ReplaceNewLine
";
$data
; 1) ]

) = 1 ]

$data
; Value: GetValue(
$data
; 1) ]

#  Process Answer
If [
$data
= "K A" ]
# Answer from keyboard turn off
#  okay
Else If [
$data
= "PMW A" ]
# Answer PMW

$data
= "K C 4" ]

Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Socket.Write
";
$sock
; "S") ]

$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Socket.WriteByte
";
$sock
; 13) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Socket.WriteByte
";
$sock
; 10) ]
#  Wait for Answer
Set Variable [
$data
; Value: "" ]
Loop
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,01 ]
Set Variable [
$newdata
; Value:
MBS
("
Socket.Read
";
$sock
; 1000) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Set Variable [
$data
; Value:
$data
&
$newData
]
End If
Exit Loop If [ Position(
$Data
; "¶"; 1; 1)>0 ]
End Loop
Set Variable [
$data
; Value:
MBS
("
Text.ReplaceNewLine
";
$data
; 1) ]
If [ ValueCount (
$data
) = 1 ]
Set Variable [
$data
; Value: GetValue(
$data
; 1) ]
End If
#  Process Answer
If [ Left(
$Data
; 3) = "S S" ]
Set Variable [
$Data
; Value: Substitute(
$Data
; ","; ".") ]
Set Variable [
$Data
; Value: Substitute(
$Data
; "."; ",") ]
Set Variable [
$Weight
; Value: GetAsNumber( Middle(
$Data
; 4; 20)) ]
If [ Position (
$data
; "kg"; 1;1) >0 ]
Set Variable [
$Weight
; Value:
$Weight
* 1000 ]
End If
Set Field [
Scale::Status
; "Weight read: " &
$Weight
]
Set Field [
Scale::WeightGemessen
;
$Weight
]
If [ (
$Weight
>=
Scale::WeightExpected
-
Scale::Tolerance
) and (
$Weight
<=
Scale::WeightExpected
+
Scale::Tolerance
) ]
Set Variable [
$finished
; Value: 1 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Thanks" ]
Exit Loop If [ 1 ]
Else
Show Custom Dialog [ "Please measure again" ]
End If
End If
Exit Loop If [
$finished
]
End If
Exit Loop If [
$finished
]
End Loop
Exit Loop If [
$finished
]
End Loop
# Enable keyboard

$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Socket.Write
";
$sock
; "K 2") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Socket.WriteByte
";
$sock
; 13) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Socket.WriteByte

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

    <Text> Open port</Text>

functions.name: Socket.DropMembership
functions.description: Removes this socket from a multicast group. Internally this uses IPPROTO_IP/IP_DROP_MEMBERSHIP option. Lasterror is set.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

parameters.name: MultiAddress
parameters.description: The IPv4 address of the gorup to join.

parameters.name: InterfaceAddress
parameters.description: The IPv4 address of the network interface to use. Can be empty for any interface.

functions.name: Socket.EnableKeepAlive
functions.description: Enables keep alive of TCP/IP socket. Once enabled, the computer will regularly send an alive message to the other side, so the TCP/IP socket doesn't time out.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

functions.name: Socket.GetBroadcast
functions.description: Queries whether broadcasts are allowed.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

functions.name: Socket.GetMulticastInterface
functions.description: Queries the interface to use for multicast. May return 0.0.0.0 to indicate all interfaces.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

functions.name: Socket.GetMulticastLoop
functions.description: Queries whether multicast packets are also sent to the sending socket.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

functions.name: Socket.GetMulticastTimeToLive
functions.description: Queries time to live for multicast data packets. Range from 0 to 255.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

functions.name: Socket.GetReceiveBufferSize
functions.description: Query receive buffer size. Default seems to be 128K.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

functions.name: Socket.GetSendBufferSize
functions.description: Query send buffer size. Default seems to be 128K.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

functions.name: Socket.GetTag
functions.description: Queries the tag value of the socket. See also Socket.SetTag . With the tag, we allow you to associate a text with the socket, for example a record ID.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

functions.name: Socket.GetTimeToLive
functions.description: Queries time to live for data packets. Range from 0 to 255.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

functions.name: Socket.IsClosed
functions.description: Checks if socket got closed. MBS Plugin may close sockets that report an error in Socket.Check . Returns 1 if closed, but not yet freed.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

functions.name: Socket.IsListening

functions.name: Socket.Listen
functions.description: Binds the socket to the port and starts listening. For UDP Socket, the socket starts receiving packets. For TCP Socket, the socket can accept connections which causes a script to be called. You set the script name with Socket.SetNewConnectionHandler . In the script you call Socket.AcceptNewConnection to get a new socket for the new connection. Be aware that FileMaker Server does not allow the plugin to operate if no script is running. So if you use sockets there, make sure a script is running or paused while you expect an answer coming in.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

parameters.name: Port
parameters.description: The port number to listen at. If port is zero, we pick a random free port number, which you can query with Socket.LocalPort function.

parameters.name: backlog
parameters.description: The back log for connection. How many pending connections can be accepted on this socket. Once you call Socket.AcceptNewConnection , an entry is removed here. Default is 3.

examples.label: Listen to a given Port:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Socket.Listen";
$$sock
; 12345)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Socket.Listen"; $$sock; 12345)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

$$sock
) = 0]
Show Custom Dialog ["Failed to create Socket";
$$sock
]

End If

$r
; Value:

("
Socket.Listen
";
$$sock
;
UDP Broadcast::Port
)]
Set Field [
UDP Broadcast::Send
Result]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Socket.SetDataAvailableHandler
";
$$sock
; Get(FileName); "ReadMessage")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Socket.SetBroadcast
";
$$sock
; 1)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Socket.SetMulticastLoop
";
$$sock
; 0)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [not (GetAsNumber($$sock) > 0)]
	Set Variable [$$sock; Value:MBS("Socket.NewUDPSocket")]
	If [GetAsNumber($$sock) = 0]
		Show Custom Dialog ["Failed to create Socket"; $$sock]

	End If
End If

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Socket.Peek
functions.description: Peeks bytes from socket and returns them as text. The text returned may be shorter than length bytes. If you like to look into the whole buffer, please use Socket.PeekAll function.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

parameters.name: Length
parameters.description: Maximum number of bytes to read.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Default is native. This function can also handle UTF-16 as well as UTF-16LE and UTF-16BE for little/big endian byte order. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

examples.label: Read up to 8 bytes and decode them from Windows text encoding into FileMaker:

=
MBS
( "
Socket.Read
";
$sock

    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$value</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

functions.name: Socket.Read
functions.description: Reads bytes from socket and returns them as text. The text returned may be shorter than length bytes. The buffer size of the socket is managed by the OS and can be several megabytes big if needed. To read the whole input buffer, use Socket.ReadAll . To look into buffer without removing text, please use Socket.Peek function instead. Please leave some time between Socket.Write calls and a subsequent reading of the results with a read function, so the other side has time to process your request. A script pause of a second or two may help.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

parameters.name: Length
parameters.description: Maximum number of bytes to read.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Default is native. This function can also handle UTF-16 as well as UTF-16LE and UTF-16BE for little/big endian byte order. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:

("Trace")]

("
Socket.Connect
";
TCP Socket::DomainOrIP
; 80)]

MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Error";
$sock
]
Exit Script []

$request
; Value:"GET "&
TCP Socket::Path
&" HTTP/
1.0
¶Host: "&
TCP Socket::DomainOrIP
&"¶¶"]

$request
; Value:

("
Text.ReplaceNewLine
";
$request
; 2)]
Set Field [
TCP Socket::RequestUsed
;
$request
]

";

;
$request
)]

$sock
; 1000)]

; Value:

";

; 1)]

;
$data
]

; Value:

Socket.Close
";
$sock
)]

examples.label: Read data and put in a new record:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Socket.Read";
$$sock
; 1500; "UTF-8")]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Error Reading";
$r
]
Else If [Length(
$r
) = 0]
Show Custom Dialog ["Error Reading"; "No data available."]
Else
New Record/Request
Set Field [
UDP Broadcast::Message
Received;
$r
]
Set Field [
UDP Broadcast::Sender
;
MBS
("
Socket.LastMessageIP
";
$$sock
)]
Commit Records/Requests [No dialog]
End If

    </Repetition>

      <Calculation>"Error Reading"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>

        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="125" name="Else If">

  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">

functions.name: Socket.ReadAll
functions.description: Reads bytes from socket and returns them as text. This function will look how much is in read buffer and query all bytes inside. May not work for SSL sockets. The buffer size of the socket is managed by the OS and can be several megabytes big if needed. To look into buffer without removing text, please use Socket.PeekAll function instead.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Default is native. This function can also handle UTF-16 as well as UTF-16LE and UTF-16BE for little/big endian byte order. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

examples.label: Read all bytes and decode them from Windows text encoding into FileMaker:
examples.text_code:
$value
=
MBS
( "Socket.ReadAll";
$sock
; "windows" )

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Socket.ReadAll"; $sock; "windows" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$value</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Socket.ReadLine
functions.description: Reads a line of data from socket and returns them as text. The delimiter allows you to read from the buffer just one line. If two lines are in read buffer, we leave second line there for the next call.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

parameters.name: Delimiter
parameters.description: Which character to use as delimiter. Pass number from 0 to 255 for the byte to use. e.g. 13 for CR line ending. Special handling is done if you pass 1310 as delimiter to cover CRLF as a 2 byte delimiter.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Default is native. This function can also handle UTF-16 as well as UTF-16LE and UTF-16BE for little/big endian byte order. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

examples.label: Read one line and decode them from Windows text encoding into FileMaker:

Socket.Read
";

    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$value</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Socket.ReadMLLP

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

parameters.name: Length
parameters.description: Maximum number of bytes to read.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Default is native. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

functions.name: Socket.RemoteIP
functions.description: Returns IP of the remote socket. Only for TCP Sockets. For UDP Sockets, pelase use Socket.LastMessageIP .

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

functions.name: Socket.RemotePort
functions.description: Returns port of the socket on the other side. Only for TCP Sockets. For UDP Sockets, pelase use Socket.LastMessagePort .

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

functions.result: Return port number or error message.

functions.name: Socket.SSL.Accept
functions.description: Wait for a TLS/SSL client to initiate a TLS/SSL handshake. Calls internally Socket.SSL.Initialize if you did not yet call it.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

functions.name: Socket.SSL.Connect
functions.description: Initiate the TLS/SSL handshake with an TLS/SSL server. Calls internally Socket.SSL.Initialize if you did not yet call it. Instead of using this SSL socket, please consider letting CURL do the job: Use CURL.SetOptionConnectOnly to let CURL connect and than send data using CURL.SendData and CURL.SendText . For receiving, please use CURL.ReceiveData and CURL.ReceiveText . This gives you much more SSL options and proxy support.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

functions.name: Socket.SSL.GetMethod
functions.description: Queries the SSL method.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

functions.name: Socket.SSL.Initialize
functions.description: Initializes a SSL connection. First you need to create TCP socket and accept or connect. Optionally you can call Socket.SSL.Prepare first. After Socket.SSL.Initialize you can call Socket.SSL.Connect or Socket.SSL.Accept . Instead of using this SSL socket, please consider letting CURL do the job: Use CURL.SetOptionConnectOnly to let CURL connect and than send data using CURL.SendData and CURL.SendText . For receiving, please use CURL.ReceiveData and CURL.ReceiveText . This gives you much more SSL options and proxy support.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

functions.name: Socket.SSL.Prepare
functions.description: First step in SSL initialization.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

functions.name: Socket.SSL.SetMethod
functions.description: Sets the SSL method for this socket. You first need to call Socket.SSL.Prepare to init SSL to the step where you can set method.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

parameters.name: Method
parameters.description: The new SSL method. Can be SSLv2, SSLv3, SSLv23, TLSv1.0, TLSv1.1, TLSv1.2 or DTLSv1.0.

functions.name: Socket.SSL.UseCertificateFile
functions.description: Defines to use a certificate key file for this SSL socket. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The path to the certificate file.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The type of the file. Can be "PEM" or "ASN1".

functions.result: Returns OK on success or error.

functions.name: Socket.SSL.UsePrivateKeyFile
functions.description: Defines to use a private key file for this SSL socket. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The path to the certificate file.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The type of the file. Can be "PEM" or "ASN1".

functions.result: Returns OK on success or error.

functions.name: Socket.SendMessage
functions.signature: MBS( "Socket.SendMessage"; SocketID; IP; Port; Text { ; Encoding } )
functions.description: Sends an UDP data message.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

parameters.name: IP
parameters.description: The target IP or name.

parameters.name: Port
parameters.description: The target port to send to.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to send.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text parameter. Default is native. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

functions.result: Returns the number of bytes written or an error message.

examples.label: Send UDP Package:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Socket.SendMessage";
$$sock
;
UDP Broadcast::BroadcastAddress
;
UDP Broadcast::Port
;
UDP Broadcast::Message
To Send; "UTF-8")]
If [GetAsNumber(
$r
) = 0]
Set Field [
UDP Broadcast::Send
Result; "Failed to Send: "&
$r
]

Result; "Sent "&
$r
&" Bytes."]

  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>

examples.label: Opens stream to Midi device and plays notes with time offsets:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$DeviceName
; Value:"Your device name"]
If [
$$stream
= ""]
Set Variable [
$$stream
; Value:
MBS
("
PortMidi.OpenOutput
";
$DeviceName
; 100; 10)]
If [
MBS
("ISError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Failed to open Device";
$$stream
]
End If
End If
#Send notes

$ts
; Value:

( "
PortMidi.TimeStamp
" )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PortMidi.SendMessage
";
$$stream
; 144; 48; 95;
$ts
)]

$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PortMidi.SendMessage
";
$$stream
; 128; 48; 0;
$ts
+ 500 )]

$r
; Value:

("
PortMidi.SendMessage
";
$$stream
; 144; 50; 95;
$ts
+ 1000 )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PortMidi.SendMessage
";
$$stream
; 128; 50; 0;
$ts
+ 1500 )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PortMidi.SendMessage
";
$$stream
; 144; 52; 95;
$ts
+ 2000 )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PortMidi.SendMessage
";
$$stream
; 128; 52; 0;
$ts
+ 2500 )]
examples.xml_code:

  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Send notes</Text>
  </Step>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PortMidi.TimeStamp" )</Calculation>

functions.name: Socket.SetDataAvailableEvaluate
functions.description: Sets the data available evaluation for this socket. When new data arrives, this expression is evaluated, so you can call Socket.Read function. Be aware that expression may be evaluated when just a few bytes are in the read buffer. So the data message you wait for may only be available partly. In that case, please read available bytes, check if whole packet/message arrived and if not, store read data for later and wait for next evaluation. In the expression the text $$ID$$ will be replaced when evaluated with the socket reference, so you can pass it to other functions if needed.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate. Can be in local language of FileMaker Pro or english. For Server must always be in english.

functions.name: Socket.SetDataAvailableHandler
functions.description: Sets the data available handler for this socket. When new data arrives, this script is called, so you can call Socket.Read function. Be aware that script may be called when just a few bytes are in the read buffer. So the data message you wait for may only be available partly. In that case, please read available bytes, check if whole packet/message arrived and if not, store read data for later and wait for next script call. The script parameter is the ID of the socket. You can associate additional values with Socket.SetTag function to the socket and use them in the script triggered. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

examples.label: Create TCP Socket listening on a port:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$$sock
; Value:
MBS
("
Socket.NewTCPSocket
")]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Failed to create Socket";
$$sock
]
Exit Script []
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Socket.SetNewConnectionHandler
";
$$sock
; Get(FileName); "NewConnection")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Socket.SetErrorHandler";
$$sock
; Get(FileName); "SocketError")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Socket.Listen
";
$$sock
;
TCP Receiver::ListenPort
)]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Failed to create Socket";
$$sock
]
Exit Script []
End If

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Socket.SetErrorHandler"; $$sock; Get(FileName); "SocketError")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Socket.Listen"; $$sock; TCP Receiver::ListenPort)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("IsError")</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Failed to create Socket"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$$sock</Calculation>

      </Button>

functions.name: Socket.SetNewConnectionEvaluate
functions.description: Sets the new connection expression for this socket. This expression is evaluated for listening TCP Socket when new connections arrive so you can call Socket.AcceptNewConnection . Be aware that FileMaker Server does not allow the plugin to operate if no script is running. So if you use sockets there, make sure a script is running or paused while you expect an answer coming in. In the expression the text $$ID$$ will be replaced when evaluated with the socket reference, so you can pass it to other functions if needed.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate. Can be in local language of FileMaker Pro or english. For Server must always be in english.

functions.name: Socket.SetNewConnectionHandler
functions.description: Sets the new connection handler for this socket. This script is called for listening TCP Socket when new connections arrive so you can call Socket.AcceptNewConnection . Be aware that FileMaker Server does not allow the plugin to operate if no script is running. So if you use sockets there, make sure a script is running or paused while you expect an answer coming in. The script parameter is the ID of the socket. You can associate additional values with Socket.SetTag function to the socket and use them in the script triggered. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run

functions.name: Socket.Write
functions.description: Writes text to the socket. Returns number of bytes sent. It may take a few milliseconds till data arrives on the other socket. Result is -1 if socket can't write. See Socket.LastError for error code. Changed in v10.4 to return error if socket is closed or disconnected instead of -1.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to send.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text parameter. Default is native. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

functions.result: Returns the number of bytes written or an error message.

$r

("Trace")]

("
Socket.Connect
";
TCP Socket::DomainOrIP
; 80)]

("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Error";
$sock
]
Exit Script []

Set Variable [
$request
; Value:"GET "&
TCP Socket::Path
&" HTTP/
1.0
¶Host: "&

&"¶¶"]

$request
; Value:

("
Text.ReplaceNewLine
";
$request
; 2)]

TCP Socket::RequestUsed
;

functions.name: Socket.WriteByte
functions.description: Writes a single byte to the socket. This is useful if you need to send single control bytes like message start or message end. Result is -1 if socket can't write. See Socket.LastError for error code. Changed in v10.4 to return error if socket is closed or disconnected instead of -1. If you send multiple bytes, it may be more efficient to prepare a hex string with all the byte values and use Socket.WriteHex function.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The byte value. Range from 0 to 255.

functions.name: Socket.WriteHex
functions.description: Writes text to the socket. You pass the text hex encoded. For example for "Hello" you pass "48656C6C6F". Result is -1 if socket can't write. See Socket.LastError for error code. Changed in v10.4 to return error if socket is closed or disconnected instead of -1.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to send in hex encoding.

functions.result: Returns the number of bytes written or an error message.

examples.label: Write some text:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Socket.WriteHex";
$sock
; "48656C6C6F" )

    </Value>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Socket.WriteMLLP
functions.description: Writes text to the socket. This function wraps the given text into a MLLP packet by adding start and end bytes. Result is -1 if socket can't write. See Socket.LastError for error code. Changed in v10.4 to return error if socket is closed or disconnected instead of -1.

parameters.name: SocketID
parameters.description: The socket ID received by Socket.Connect function.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to send.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text parameter. Default is native. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

functions.result: Returns the number of bytes written or an error message.

examples.label: Write something:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Socket.WriteMLLP";
$sock
; "Hello World" )

functions.name: SSH.CloseChannel
functions.description: Closes the channel. This closes it now. Normally all channels are closed automatically when you release connection with SSH.Release .

parameters.name: SSH
parameters.description: The SSH session reference number from the plugin.

parameters.name: Wait
parameters.description: Whether to wait if it takes longer. Default is 0.

functions.name: SSH.ConfigureKeepAlive
functions.description: Configures keep alive probes. Set how often keepalive messages should be sent.

parameters.name: SSH
parameters.description: The SSH session reference number from the plugin.

parameters.name: WantReply
parameters.description: WantReply indicates whether the keepalive messages should request a response from the server. Default value is true for this parameter.

parameters.name: Interval
parameters.description: Interval is number of seconds that can pass without any I/O, use 0 to disable keepalives. To avoid some busy-loop corner-cases, if you specify an interval of 1 it will be treated as 2. Default is 2.

functions.name: SSH.Connect
functions.signature: MBS( "SSH.Connect"; SSH; IP { ; Port; TimeOut; ProtocolPreference } )
functions.description: Connects a new socket to the given IP & Port. Timeout defines timeout for connection in seconds. While waiting, the plugin yields time to other threads. Version 6.3 now supports passing IPv4, iPv6, or a host name.

parameters.name: SSH
parameters.description: The SSH session reference number from the plugin.

parameters.name: IP
parameters.description: The IP to connect to.

parameters.name: Port
parameters.description: The port number to connect to. Default is 22 which is standard port for ssh.

parameters.name: TimeOut
parameters.description: The connection timeout in seconds for the TCP/IP connection. Default is 30 seconds.

parameters.name: ProtocolPreference
parameters.description: Pass 4 to connect via IPv4. Pass 6 to connect via IPv6. Default is to try whatever the DNS suggests first.

examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "SSH.Connect";
$ssh
; "
192.168
.
2.1
" )]

examples.label: Full example:
examples.text_code:
# Start a new SSH session
Set Variable [
$ssh
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.New
" )]
# Connect to an IP
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "SSH.Connect";
$ssh
;
SSH::Host
;
SSH::Port
)]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Connect error";
$r
]
Else
# Run the session handshake.
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.SessionHandshake
";
$ssh
)]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Connect error";
$r
]
Else
# We can show the hostkey. You can compare it to known hostkey.
Set Field [
SSH::HostKeyHash
;
MBS
( "
SSH.HostKey
";
$ssh
)]
# We query supported authentication ways
Set Variable [
$authuserlist
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.UserAuthList
";
$ssh
;
SSH::Username
)]
Set Field [
SSH::UserAuthList
;
$authuserlist
]
Set Variable [
$pos1
; Value:Position (
$authuserlist
; "password"; 1; 1 )]
Set Variable [
$pos2
; Value:Position (
$authuserlist
; "keyboard-interactive"; 1; 1 )]
Set Variable [
$pos3
; Value:Position (
$authuserlist
; "publickey"; 1; 1 )]
If [
$pos1
> 0]
# Authenticate with password
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.UserAuthPassword
";
$ssh
;
SSH::Username
;
SSH::Password
)]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Connect error";
$r
]
End If
Else If [
$pos2
> 0]
# Authenticate with answering question. Plugin passes password for answer.
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.UserAuthKeyboardInteractive
";
$ssh
;
SSH::Username
;
SSH::Password
)]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Connect error";
$r
]
End If
Else If [
$pos3
> 0]
# Authenticate with public key file
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.UserAuthPublicKey
";
$ssh
;
SSH::Username
; "your key path"; "your key path";
SSH::Password
)]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Connect error";
$r
]
End If
End If
If [
MBS
( "
SSH.IsAuthenticated
";
$ssh
)  ≠ 1]
Show Custom Dialog ["Authentication"; "no authenticated. Supported ways: " &
$authuserlist
]
Else
# Start a new channel
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.OpenSession
";
$ssh
)]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Connect error";
$r
]
Else
# Execute a command
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.Execute
";
$ssh
;
SSH::Command
)]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Connect error";
$r
]
Else
# We read text in several chunks until no new text is coming any more:
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:""]
Loop
Pause/Resume Script [Duration (seconds): ,1]
# Read some text:
Set Variable [
$newtext
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.ReadText
";
$ssh
; 10000; "UTF8" )]
If [
MBS
("iserror") = 0]
If [Length(
$newText
) > 0]
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:
$text
&
$newText
]
End If
Exit Loop If [Length(
$newText
) = 0]
End If
End Loop
Set Field [
SSH::Result
;
$text
]
End If
End If
# Close channel
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "SSH.Close";
$ssh
)]
# Disconnect
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.Disconnect
";
$ssh
)]
End If
End If
End If
# Release memory.
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.Release
";
$ssh
)]

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>

    </Title>

    </Message>
    <Buttons>
